data.title: VEON 1Q Ebitda $439M >VEON
BOJ expected to pause rate hikes through September amid tariffs uncertainty - poll
data.description:
• 59 of 62 economists (95%) see no change to interest rates in June meeting
  • 39 of 58 economists (67%) see interest rates staying unchanged in Q3 (previously 36%)
  • 30 of 58 economists (52%) see at least one more rate hike by year-end
  • The median prediction for end-September rate is now 0.50% (previously 0.75%)
  • The median prediction for year-end rate is 0.75% (unchanged)
  • 16 of 29 economists (55%) either strongly or somewhat approve of the Japanese government's approach to tariff negotiations with the US

data.title: Australia's Xero rises on higher full-year profit
data.description:
Shares of Australia's Xero rose to its strongest level in nearly three months on Thursday after the accounting software provider reported a 30% rise in annual net profit.

The New Zealand-based firm posted a net profit after tax of NZ$227.8 million ($134.5 million) for fiscal year 2025, up from NZ$174.6 million a year earlier.

($1 = 1.6938 New Zealand dollars)

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on May 15
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Thursday.

The lira was quoted at 38.77 against the U.S. dollar at 0400 GMT, unchanged from its close on Thursday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed slightly higher at 9,701.55 points on Thursday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Stocks struggled for direction on Thursday while the dollar stumbled as the euphoria from market tailwinds earlier in the week fizzled out, with traders looking to U.S. data later in the day for further catalysts.

ERDOGAN SCHEDULE

President Tayyip Erdogan will welcome Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy in Ankara and hold bilateral talks (1000 GMT). Erdogan will then attend an event of his Ak Party and business group later in the evening.

NATO FM MEETING

NATO foreign ministers arrive for informal meeting and make doorstep remarks to media.

FIDAN NEWS CONFERENCE

Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan will hold a press conference following an informal meeting of NATO foreign ministers. Fidan is scheduled to meet with the Germany, France, U.S. and Syrian counterparts.

RUSSIA-UKRAINE TALKS

Russia and Ukraine are set to hold their first direct, high-level talks in Istanbul in more than three years of war, seeking a possible ceasefire to the deadliest conflict in Europe since World War Two.

VICE PRESIDENT

Turkey's Vice President Cevdet Yilmaz visits Uzbekistan, attends business and economy events.

CENTRAL BANK

Turkish central bank will announce statistics on net, gross reserves and data on stock, bond holding of investors (1130 GMT).

BUDGET BALANCE

The Ministry of Treasury and Finance will publish the April budget balance data. (0800 GMT)

For other related news, double-click on:

data.title: JYP Entertainment's Share Price Could Be Resilient — Market Talk
data.description: JYP Entertainment's share price could be resilient partly due to support from its new artists, Daiwa Capital analysts Joon Lee and Thomas Y. Kwon write in a note. Current weak fundamentals at the K-Pop label could turn around in 2026 on the likely bigger revenue contribution from the seven-member boy band KickFlip and other new talents, they say. They cut their 2025 net-profit forecast for JYP by 32% after a larger-than-expected artist fee increase for its flagship boy band Stray Kids hurt 1Q earnings. Still, Daiwa raises the stock's target price by 5.7% to KRW74,000 and keeps an outperform rating. Shares are 6.7% higher at KRW73,700. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Westpac's Mortgage-Covered Bonds Receive 'AAA' Rating From Fitch
data.description:
Fitch Ratings assigned Thursday Westpac Banking's 1.5 billion euros of series 2025-C1 mortgage-covered bonds an "AAA" rating with a stable outlook.

The covered bond rating is based on the bank's long-term issuer default rating, the various uplifts above the rating granted to the program, and the over-collateralization protection provided through the program's asset percentage, the credit ratings agency said.

This issuance brings the bank's total outstanding covered bonds to the equivalent of AU$31.5 billion.

The fixed-rate bonds are due in May 2030 with a 12-month extendable maturity.

Westpac Banking's shares rose nearly 1% in Australia in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: US 10-Year Yield Holds Advance
data.description:
The yield on the 10-year US Treasury note held steady around 4.53% on Thursday, maintaining recent gains as easing global trade tensions dampened demand for safe-haven assets.

Investor sentiment was supported by the mutual tariff reductions announced by the US and China, set to last for 90 days, easing fears that escalating trade barriers could tip the US economy into recession.

Additionally, Beijing’s decision to lift its export ban on rare earths targeting US entities signaled further de-escalation in the trade standoff.

Currently, markets are pricing in just two Federal Reserve interest rate cuts by end of year instead of four reductions priced in last week.

This was despite a softer-than-expected CPI print for April.

Investors now look ahead to US retail sales and producer inflation data on Thursday for fresh insight on the economy.

data.title: Kyivstar-parent VEON reports quarterly revenue growth, confirms 2025 outlook
data.description:
VEON , reported a 9% growth in its quarterly revenue on Thursday, supported by increasingly robust direct digital revenue growth.

The parent of Ukraine's Kyivstar and Bangladesh's Banglalink said its revenue was $1 billion in the first quarter, with an EBITDA growth of 13.7% in reported currency to $439 million.

The group confirmed its outlook for 2025.

data.title: VEON 1Q Rev $1.03B >VEON
Enagás Closes $17 Million Stake Deal in Mexico
data.description:
Enagás completed the sale of its entire stake in Mexico-based Estación de Compresión Soto La Marina to Esentia Anáhuac Holding for $17 million.

The Spanish energy company expects the deal to generate net capital gains of 5.1 million euros, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: Aeroports de Paris Reports Higher Passenger Traffic in April
data.description:
Aeroports de Paris on Wednesday reported an increase in passenger traffic across all the group's operated airports.

The French international airport operator catered to 30,644,607 passengers in April, an increase of 7.6% year over year.

Aircraft movement across the group-operated airports came in at 199,490 in April, marking a 6.2% growth from the same year-ago month.

data.title: JYP Entertainment Target Price Raised to KRW74,000 From KRW70,000 by Daiwa Capital >035900.KQ
Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0355 GMT - Copper is lower on some profit taking following the base metal's recent price surge. The ascent was driven by the easing of trade tensions between the U.S. and its trading partners. However, copper prices may remain supported in the near term as the U.S.-China trade deal will likely drive demand higher, Maike Futures analysts say in a commentary. The three-month LME copper contract is 0.7% lower at $9,535.00 a ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0239 GMT - Palm oil prices are lower in Asian trade, tracking weakness in soybean oil on the Chicago Board of Trade, says David Ng, a trader at Kuala Lumpur-based Iceberg X. Persistent concerns over rising output and stock levels are also seen weighing on sentiment, he says. CPO production for May 1-10 is estimated to have risen 22.3% on month, according to Malaysia's Southern Peninsular Palm Oil Millers' Association. Ng sees support at 3,750 ringgit a ton and resistance at 3,980 ringgit a ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for July delivery is down by 25 ringgit to 3,898 ringgit a ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2347 GMT - Gold is higher in the early Asian session on a possible technical recovery after gold futures fell to their lowest close in over a month on Wednesday. Gold prices slipped overnight on hopes of easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East after President Trump announced he would lift economic sanctions on Syria, Kudotrade's Konstantinos Chrysikos says in an email. However, gold might find some support from softer U.S. inflation data released earlier this week, the head of Customer Relationship Management says. The lower inflation reading supports the case for Fed rate cuts, which would favor non-yielding assets like gold, Chrysikos adds. Spot gold is 0.3% higher at $3,184.84/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1959 GMT - Lean hog futures on the CME fall 0.7% to 98.775 cents a pound. Hogs fell after three consecutive days of closing higher — coinciding with the 'Goldman roll,' or monthly rebalancing of the Goldman Sachs Commodity Index. Activity seems to indicate that Goldman Sachs anticipates hog futures to appreciate in value this month. "We gained a serious 11K contracts of open interest yesterday alone and nearly 20K in the five days of the Goldman roll, which might indicate some index fund traders adding length," StoneX says in a note. Live cattle futures close down 1.1% to $2.139 a pound. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1949 GMT - Gold futures fell today, making it the fourth lower session out of the past six and falling to its lowest close in over a month. Front-month gold closes down 1.8% to $3,181.40 a troy ounce. It's another sizable loss for gold, which is representative of fund traders cutting "another gigantic chunk of length from the gold spec position," says Robert Yawger of Mizuho Securities USA in a note. Yawger adds that gold has room to keep moving lower, potentially retesting the $3,000 a troy ounce level for the first time since April. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1946 GMT - Crude futures settle lower after four straight sessions of gains, with the market seeing a bearish tilt in the unexpected 3.5 million barrel U.S. crude inventory build and OPEC keeping its demand growth estimates unchanged for this year and next while returning barrels to the market at an accelerated pace. The U.S. crude build "wasn't as negative as widely perceived," Ritterbusch says, noting most of the increase was in the West Coast region and the 1.1 million barrel draw at Cushing, the Nymex delivery hub. While strong refinery activity would be bullish for crude demand, "we look for this element to be offset by ongoing increases in OPEC+ production that will likely be hiking crude imports while, at the same time, backing out some exports," the firm adds. WTI settles down 0.8% at $63.15 a barrel, and Brents falls 0.8% to $66.09. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1907 GMT - U.S. natural gas futures retreat as the market prepares for another large inventory build in tomorrow's EIA report to increase the surplus over the five-year average. "And it doesn't help that LNG exports are softer today at under 15 Bcf barring higher afternoon revisions," NatGasWeather.com says in a note. On the bullish side are high temperatures in Texas that will drive up electricity demand, although wind and solar generation have been strong the past few days, the forecaster adds. Analysts in a Wall Street Journal survey estimate a 111 Bcf increase in natural gas storage for last week, increasing the surplus to 58 Bcf from 30 Bcf the week before. Nymex natural gas settles down 4.3% to $3.492/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1834 GMT - Concerns about higher OPEC production has likely kept oil from joining the rally in growth-sensitive metals, while fears of collateral damage to demand from an "imaginary recession" are overdone, says Vincent Deluard, director of global macro strategy at StoneX. He shrugs off concerns about a Saudi-led price war with the decision by OPEC+ to step up its pace of production increases, noting the group's reiteration that the moves could be paused or reversed. "Iraq and Kazakhstan were overproducing, and they couldn't do much about it," he says. "If they wanted to maintain credibility, they had to change the target and that's probably what they did." A rise in summer demand, including travel in the Middle East for the Haj pilgrimage, is also likely behind OPEC's positive view of demand, Deluard adds. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1831 GMT - CBOT wheat is the lead gainer among CBOT futures today, which is being attributed to a wave of short-covering by fund traders. Charlie Sernatinger of Marex says in a note it's "a long ride down off of commercial talk that the USDA wheat yields were too generous," following the WASDE report Monday. Both hard red and soft red winter wheats are carrying large short positions among fund traders, the CFTC said in its last Commitment of Traders report. CBOT wheat is up 1.5% late in trading. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1825 GMT - 60% of soybeans grown in the U.S. are exported to other nations, but American farmers are looking for ways to decrease their reliance on export markets, executives with the United Soybean Board tell WSJ in an interview. One way that has farmers excited is by expanding the usage of soybean oil as an ingredient in industrial production and goods--not only for alternative fuels but for the making of other consumer products as well, particularly ones that in the past may have been made using plastics derived from petroleum. "I think we've got the answer to that," says Philip Good, chair of the board and a farmer of row crops and livestock in Mississippi. CBOT soybean futures are up 0.4% in late trading. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1754 GMT - The dollar remains overvalued by 5% to 10% against a broad basket of currencies, First Eagle's Idanna Appio says citing a proprietary model. She says higher interest rates in the U.S. than in other developed economies "are still supporting the dollar." "You could see some more rebalancing away from from U.S. assets," toward equities, currencies or bonds that are cheaper, she says. A weakening of the dollar, however, won't necessarily mean its reserve currency status is fading. "You could still see a weakness in the dollar just from sort of normal cyclical factors as opposed to some big shift away from the reserve currency status." The WSJ Dollar Index is flat. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1732 GMT - Soybean farmers contend that attacks from Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. targeting supposed health issues around seed oils like soybean oil miss the mark. Farmers overwhelmingly agree that Americans should consume more natural foods, says Lucas Lentsch, CEO of the United Soybean Board--but says that soybean oil is part of that category. "We're fully supportive of having a food dialogue, let's just make sure that science stays at the center of that," says Lentsch. The USB cites studies showing health benefits for these oils, including a study carried out by Harvard published this year. Kennedy campaigned on the idea that vegetable oil consumption leads to health issues like chronic inflammation, advocating for increased use of products like beef tallow as part of his "Make America Healthy Again" initiative. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: EU Regulators Likely to Accept Microsoft Deal to Settle Complaints on Office, Teams Products
data.description:
Regulators in the European Union are likely to accept Microsoft's settlement deal for complaints against its Office and Teams products, Reuters reported Wednesday, citing three people familiar with the matter.

The unnamed sources noted that before making a final decision, the European Commission is expected to launch in the coming months a consultation process with rivals and customers of the US tech group.

Microsoft and the commission did not immediately respond to requests for comments from MT Newswires.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Trade Deal Rally Fades; Earnings, Econ Data in Focus
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

EU GDP, industrial production; U.K. GDP, industrial production, trade, business investment; trading updates from Siemens, Thyssenkrupp, Allianz, Deutsche Telekom, RWE, Merck KGaA, 3i Group, Engie, Aviva, ITV, Sage Group, National Grid, CVC Capital Partners

Opening Call:

European stock futures little changed with broadly negative bias tracking declines in Asian stock benchmarks early Thursday; the dollar edged lower; Treasury yields steadied; oil and gold futures declined.

Equities:

European stock futures were treading water with a mostly negative bias as enthusiasm over trade deals wears off. However, hopes for easing geopolitical tensions after President Trump announced lifting of economic sanctions on Syria could lend some support.

Artificial intelligence stocks lifted the broader U.S. markets on Wednesday. Steven Blitz, chief U.S. economist at GlobalData TS Lombard, said he wasn't surprised when Trump opted for a "90-day hiatus" on China tariffs, given the scope of the recent selloff in stocks and bonds. But he remains skeptical that investors can count on another pivot from the Trump administration on trade once a new budget bill gets passed.

U.S. economic data is in focus today with the producer price index and retail sales providing another look at how tariff policy impacted the economy in April. With Wall Street still riding the high of the weekend's U.S.-China trade talks, an update on how things are actually holding up could give the market a jolt.

Fed Chairman Jerome Powell will also speak at the Thomas Laubach Research Conference shortly before the market opens.

Forex:

The dollar remains overvalued by 5% to 10% against a broad basket of currencies, First Eagle's Idanna Appio said citing a proprietary model. Higher interest rates in the U.S. than in other developed economies "are still supporting the dollar." "You could see some more rebalancing away from from U.S. assets," toward equities, currencies or bonds that are cheaper, she said.

A weakening of the dollar, however, won't necessarily mean its reserve currency status is fading. "You could still see a weakness in the dollar just from sort of normal cyclical factors as opposed to some big shift away from the reserve currency status."

Bonds:

The U.S.'s gaping budget hole means Treasury yields will trend higher, said Bank of America's Mark Capleton. He points to data indicating less interest from foreign investors to finance the U.S. deficit.

Decreasing demand for Treasurys would depress their price, pushing yields higher. BofA forecasts a $2 trillion deficit this year, expanding to $2.3 trillion by 2027.

These are numbers that we expect to weigh on long-dated Treasurys, Capleton said.

Energy:

Oil fell early Thursday amid a surprise increase in U.S. crude inventories. The American Petroleum Institute reported a 4.29-million-barrel increase in crude stocks last week, contradicting market expectations of a 2.4-million-barrel decrease, Pepperstone's Quasar Elizundia said.

"This unexpected increase set a bearish tone that was later confirmed by the official EIA data," the research strategist added.

According to the EIA, commercial crude oil stocks excluding the Strategic Petroleum Reserve rose by 3.5 million barrels in the week ended May 9.

Metals:

Gold gave up early gains to turn lower in Asian trade. Gold prices slipped overnight on hopes of easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East after President Trump announced he would lift economic sanctions on Syria, Kudotrade's Konstantinos Chrysikos said.

However, gold might find some support from softer U.S. inflation data released earlier this week. The lower inflation reading supports the case for Fed rate cuts, which would favor non-yielding assets like gold, Chrysikos added.

--

Copper fell on some profit-taking. Prices are likely to be supported near-term as the U.S. trade agreement will drive demand higher, Maike Futures analysts said.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Trump Meets New Syrian Leader After Announcing End of Sanctions

RIYADH, Saudi Arabia-President Trump met Syria's new president here after announcing that he would lift crippling economic sanctions on the war-torn country and signing a bevy of Saudi investment deals on the first day of his Middle East tour.

Trump shook hands with Ahmed al-Sharaa, who also embraced Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, in photos released after the meeting that the White House said lasted 33 minutes. A U.S. spokesman said it was the first encounter between presidents of the two countries in 25 years.

Powell to Speak on Fed's Future. What to Watch.

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell will take the stage Thursday morning for his first public remarks since last week's policy meeting, where officials kept interest rates unchanged for the third consecutive time. But investors expecting fresh clues on the timing of rate cuts may be looking in the wrong place.

Instead, Powell is likely to focus on something potentially more consequential: the Fed's review of its monetary policy framework, the blueprint that guides how the central bank interprets its dual mandate of maximum employment and price stability.

How Qatar Spent Billions to Gain Influence in the U.S.

Qatar's potential plan to provide a $400 million jumbo jet to the U.S. to use as Air Force One underscores how the tiny Gulf state has managed to diplomatically punch above its weight for years: It has a lot of money and is willing to spend it.

The country's ruling monarchy has showered billions of dollars derived from its vast natural gas reserves on U.S. institutions, mainly the military and universities, while ramping up spending on lobbyists to tilt policy in its favor.

Boeing, GE Aerospace Get Qatar Airways Order After Saudi AI Tech Deals

Boeing and GE Aerospace received a $96 billion order from Qatar Airways as President Trump looks to boost investments in defense and aircraft in the Middle East after reaching artificial intelligence and tech agreements on Tuesday.

The order, announced Wednesday, includes up to 210 American-made Boeing 787 Dreamliners and 777X aircraft and more than 400 GEnx and GE9X engines from GE Aerospace. The sales figure doesn't appear to include discounts of as much as 50% to 60%, which are typical for jet deals.

Alibaba Stock Is Up 60% This Year. Earnings Will Test the Rally.

Alibaba Group has been on a tear, with the stock up double-digits this year. Earnings could make or break the Chinese online retailer's rally.

Walmart Is Retail's Bellwether for Tariffs. What Earnings Will Show.

Walmart, as the world's largest retailer, has a center-row seat to the hottest ticket in town-the tariffs drama. And it has a lot at stake: sales and earnings.

The discount chain's first-quarter report comes out Thursday morning and the numbers will be an early look at how the shifts in U.S. trade policy are affecting American shoppers and, in turn, company profits.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Ixigo parent India's Le Travenues jumps after quarterly profit rise
data.description:
** Shares of India's Le Travenues Tech climbs 7% to 180 rupees

** Stock set to gain for the seventh straight session, if trends hold

** Parent of travel booking platform, ixigo, posted 128% y/y jump in Q4 profit after tax, 72% rise in rev

** YTD LETR is up 4% vs 22% decline in rival EaseMyTrip-parent Easy Trip Planners

data.title: Al Ashghal Al Moysra's Board Proposes Statutory Reserve Balance Transfer
data.description:
Al Ashghal Al Moysra's (SASE:9608) board proposed to transfer the balance of the statutory reserve to retained earnings.

The transfer concerns the logistics and facility management services company's balance totaled 15,000 Saudi riyals as per its financial statements ending Dec. 31, 2024, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: 2025 Brand Global Communication Conference held in Shanghai
data.description:
SHANGHAI, May 15, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — On May 12, the 2025 Brand Global Communication Conference was held in Shanghai, highlighting the theme of "Brands bring better future for the world." More than 100 representatives from well-known domestic enterprises, government agencies, and academic institutions gathered to discuss and analyze the going-global of Chinese brands, aiming to enhance the global influence of domestic brands entering overseas markets.

A Media Snippet accompanying this announcement is available by clicking on this link.

A report on global brand communication capability of Chinese brands was released at the conference. The report provides reference pathways for promoting high-quality brand going-global and effectively presenting the story of Chinese brands.

Since 2024, urban culture, finance, and the gaming and toy sectors have shown significant progress in brand development, industry innovation, and international communication. The report has focused on these three key areas, introducing a global communication capability ranking of Chinese brands in the specific realms.

Notably, the report marks the first in-depth regional analysis by unveiling the 2025 Shanghai cultural brand global communication capability ranking. The top ten brands encompass key sectors, including cultural tourism, design, film and television, technology, and urban intellectual property. Shortlisted brands such as the Shanghai International Film Festival, World Artificial Intelligence Conference, Shanghai International Design Week, and World Cities Day prominently illustrate the outstanding performance of Shanghai's cultural brands in the global public discourse, social media landscape, and industrial ecosystem.

This is the third consecutive year that the conference unveiled an edition of the research report on the global communication capacity of brands. The report focuses on the overall ranking and changing characteristics of Chinese brands' global communication capability over the past three years, compiling data from the 2023 and 2024 rankings while highlighting the ascendancy and contribution of Chinese brands in global communication during this period. The data indicates strong performances in the automotive, artificial intelligence, and consumer electronics sectors.

The conference is launched by the office of Xinhua Brand Reimagine, the Shanghai Branch of Xinhua News Agency, and the News & Information Center of Xinhua News Agency, with the News & Information Center's Shanghai branch and China News Development Co., Ltd. Shanghai Branch serving as organizers.

Source: China News Development Co., Ltd. Shanghai Branch

Contact person: Ms. Liu, Tel: 86-10-63074558

data.title: US poised to cut capital requirements for banks, FT reports
data.description:
Authorities in the United States are preparing to announce cuts to banks' capital requirements, the Financial Times reported on Thursday.

Regulators were in the next few months poised to reduce the supplementary leverage ratio, the newspaper reported, citing several people familiar with the matter.
